free_text: Deuteron-induced reactions on Yb: Measured cross
sections and rationale for production pathways of
Natural Yb foils 30 microns thick and natural Ti 25
microns foils for beam monitoring from Goodfellow.
Standard stacked foil irradiation method was used.
Two stacks were irradiated with incident deuteron
Long measurements of more than 24 h duration,
to assay the long-lived radionuclides, were performed
8 months after EOB at a distance of 5 cm.
Energy degradation in the stacks was estimated
by calculation using the stopping power data and
polynomial approximation of Andersen and Ziegler.
Samples were measured using HPGE detectors,
first at large detector-sample distances 50 cm.

The uncertainty in the primary beam energy,
estimated to be 0.2 MeV. The overall uncertainty in
the mean energy at the last foil in the stack was
estimated to be 0.7 MeV, taking into account
cumulative effects of the possible variations in
incident energy and number of target atoms.
